name: edit-old-string-must-replace-entire-comment-block
hook: Edit tool: old_string must include full orphaned comment text
body:
When replacing a JSDoc block header with the Edit tool, the `old_string` must include the ENTIRE old comment block — all orphaned ` *` continuation lines up to and including the closing `*/`. If `old_string` only matches the opening lines, the remaining body text stays in the file as orphaned ` *` lines after the new `*/`, causing cascading TS1161 "Unterminated regular expression literal" errors across all subsequent files in the compilation order.

**Why:** The TypeScript parser treats ` *` at column 1 as the start of a regex literal, not a comment. An orphaned ` *` line breaks the parser for the rest of the file (and sometimes subsequent files).

**How to apply:** When using Edit to replace a JSDoc header, always include the full old comment text in `old_string` — or better yet, replace the entire old block with `new_string` that contains only the new header.
why: The Edit tool's `old_string` only replaced the first N lines of a JSDoc block, leaving orphaned comment body text that TypeScript parsed as code, breaking typecheck for multiple files.
